transactions.name: Bradley Chubb
transactions.analysis: Bradley Chubb’s time in Miami showed exactly how impactful he could be when healthy, bringing energy, leadership, and a relentless edge presence to the defense. After arriving in a big midseason trade, he became a tone-setter and delivered a standout 2023 season that helped elevate the pass rush. Injuries made things tougher later on, but his effort and commitment never wavered. As the team turns the page, he leaves as a respected veteran who gave everything he had every time he stepped on the field.

transactions.name: Tyreek Hill
transactions.analysis: Tyreek Hill’s time in Miami started with the signature explosiveness and game-breaking speed fans expected, lighting up the field with electric plays that kept defenses on edge. Unfortunately, injuries and off-field distractions limited his impact later on, preventing him from consistently reaching his elite potential. Despite the setbacks, he delivered flashes of brilliance that reminded everyone of the talent he brought to the offense.

transactions.name: James Daniels
transactions.analysis: James Daniels’ stint in Miami never materialized as hoped. Healthy for just three plays, he failed to make the impact the team needed on the offensive line, leaving coaches and fans frustrated. Though brief, his time with the Dolphins serves as a cautionary tale about fit and opportunity, and the team will look elsewhere to anchor the trenches moving forward.

transactions.name: Nick Westbrook-Ikhine
transactions.analysis: Despite playing the full season, Nick Westbrook-Ikhine struggled to make a mark, recording just 11 receptions for 89 yards. While he brought professionalism and effort, the on-field production never matched expectations, and he leaves Miami as a player who simply couldn't find his rhythm in the offense.
